summarization.a: The  polar chart  shows the spatial distribution of Al₂O₃ etch rate across a Si wafer during ALE. The etch rate is highest at the wafer center (~0.139 nm/cycle) and slightly decreases toward the edge (~0.131 nm/cycle), indicating a uniformity.

a.question: What process oriented parameters  led to the cause the center-to-edge etch rate gradient observed on the wafer?
a.answer:
1   - Plasma density distribution
2  - Neutral radical flux and gas flow pattern 
3  - Ion energy/sheath potential variation

a.question: How does the etch rate non-uniformity change from the center of the wafer to the edge?
a.answer: At the center (0–1 cm): ~0.137–0.139 nm/cycle; highest etch rates. At the mid-radius (1–2.2 cm): ~0.134–0.136 nm/cycle; moderate etch rates finally at the edge (2.2–3.5 cm): ~0.131–0.133 nm/cycle; lowest etch rates

a.question: What is the minimum and maximum etch rate value shown on this given polar plot?
Is the etch-rate uniformity shown in this ALE wafer map acceptable for manufacturing nanoscale devices?
a.answer:
No

Reason - the center-to-edge variation (~0.131–0.139 nm/cycle) and the six-fold spatial nonuniformity exceed typical nanoscale CD tolerances and would likely require process or hardware correction before manufacturing.
